commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then he said, What gravestone is that that I see. And the men of the city told him, It is the sepulcher of the man of God, who came from Judah, and proclaimed these things that you have done against the altar of Beth-el. Hebrew tsiun, "an eminence "of "dry "earth, (Ezechiel xxxix. ) heaped upon a corpse; whence the Latin tumulus. (Servius) (Calmet) It seems some inscription was still to be seen on the tomb. (Menochius) Thou Septuagint, "which he proclaimed against the altar.
